content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "document-management",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 35,
    "domain_depth": 30,
    "data_richness": 25,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 5,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 30,
    "typescript_quality": 45,
    "error_handling": 25,
    "realtime": 10,
    "accessibility": 15,
    "security": 35,
    "differentiation": 10,
    "enterprise_readiness": 20
  },
  "overall_score": 23,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "getDrawings has no loading, error, or empty state handling in the consuming page",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The Drawings page likely renders raw data from getDrawings without skeleton loaders during fetch, no error boundary or inline error state on failure, and no contextual empty state when zero drawings exist. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires skeleton loaders within 100ms, contextual empty states with CTA, and inline retry on error.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the main Drawings page component: Add three states — loading, error, and empty. BEFORE: The component either shows nothing or a generic spinner while data loads, shows no error UI on fetch failure, and shows no contextual empty state. AFTER: (1) While loading, render a grid of 6 skeleton cards matching the drawing card dimensions (aspect-ratio 4:3, border-radius 12px, pulse animation). (2) On error, render an inline alert with the error message and a 'Retry' button that re-triggers the fetch. (3) When data loads successfully but the array is empty, render an empty state with a FileText icon (from lucide-react), the message 'No drawings uploaded yet. Upload your plans to enable digital markup, RFI linking, and AI coordination analysis.', and a primary 'Upload Drawings' CTA button. Ensure all three states use the existing design tokens (bg-white, border border-gray-200, rounded-xl). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "No file upload workflow exists for drawings",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA mandates drag-and-drop or file picker upload supporting PDF/DWG/DXF with processing indicators. Currently the API layer (getDrawings) is read-only with no upload mutation. The page has no upload UI at all, making the entire drawings module non-functional for content creation.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the main Drawings page component: Add a file upload trigger and modal. BEFORE: There is no upload button or drag-and-drop zone; users cannot add drawings. AFTER: (1) Add an 'Upload Drawings' button (primary style, Upload icon from lucide-react) in the page header next to any existing filter controls. (2) When clicked, open a modal dialog containing a drag-and-drop zone (dashed border, 200px min-height, accepts .pdf,.dwg,.dxf). (3) The drop zone should show 'Drag and drop drawing files here, or click to browse' with accepted formats listed below. (4) On file selection, show the filename and a simulated progress bar (since actual Supabase storage upload may not be wired yet, show a disabled state with 'Upload functionality coming soon' message). (5) Add aria-label='Upload drawings' on the button. This is a UI scaffold — the actual upload mutation will be wired in a subsequent cycle. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Drawing cards and table rows lack ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The Drawings page renders drawing items without role='listitem', aria-labels on interactive elements, or keyboard focus management. The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA global standards require axe-core zero critical violations, keyboard navigability with visible focus rings, and screen reader support on all interactive elements.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the component that renders drawing cards or list rows: Add ARIA attributes and keyboard support. BEFORE: Drawing items render as plain divs with onClick handlers but no role, aria-label, tabIndex, or keyboard event handling. AFTER: (1) Wrap the drawing list in a container with role='list' and aria-label='Project drawings'. (2) Each drawing card/row gets role='listitem', tabIndex={0}, aria-label={`Drawing ${drawing.sheetNumber || drawing.title}, ${drawing.disciplineLabel}, revision ${drawing.currentRevision?.revision_number || 0}`}. (3) Add onKeyDown handler: Enter/Space opens the drawing detail (same as click). (4) Add focus-visible ring styles: 'focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-orange-500 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-none'. (5) Status indicators (discipline color dots) get aria-hidden='true' since the discipline is conveyed in the aria-label text. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Drawings page has no responsive layout — grid does not collapse on mobile",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires every page to be usable at 375px width with no horizontal scroll or overlapping elements. The drawings grid/table likely uses a fixed multi-column layout that breaks on mobile. Drawing viewer needs pinch-to-zoom and a collapsible toolbar.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the main Drawings page component: Make the layout responsive. BEFORE: The drawing grid uses a fixed column count (e.g., grid-cols-3 or grid-cols-4) that causes horizontal overflow on mobile screens. Filter controls may also overflow. AFTER: (1) Change the grid to use responsive breakpoints: 'grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 gap-4'. (2) On mobile (< 640px), drawing cards should be full-width with a horizontal layout (thumbnail left, info right) instead of vertical stacking. (3) Filter/toolbar controls should wrap: use 'flex flex-wrap gap-2' instead of a fixed row. (4) The page header with title and upload button should use 'flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:justify-between gap-3'. (5) Add padding: 'px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No discipline filter tabs or revision history UI for drawings",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for Drawings requires discipline filter pills (Architectural blue, Structural red, etc.) with toggle behavior, revision history timeline per sheet, and linked RFI/submittal counts. None of these exist. The getDrawings API returns discipline data and revisions but the page does not expose filtering or revision browsing.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the main Drawings page component: Add discipline filter pills and revision count display. BEFORE: All drawings are shown in a flat list with no filtering by discipline. Revision data from the API is not displayed. AFTER: (1) Above the drawing grid, render a row of filter pills for each unique discipline found in the data. Each pill shows the discipline name and a count badge. Use the discipline colors from the API (Architectural=#3B82F6, Structural=#E74C3C, Mechanical=#F47820, Electrical=#F5A623, Plumbing=#4EC896, Civil=#8B5CF6). Active pills are filled with their color; inactive pills are outlined. Clicking toggles the filter. Multiple can be active simultaneously. (2) On each drawing card, show 'Rev {currentRevision.revision_number}' badge and '{revisions.length} revisions' text if revisions.length > 1. (3) Add a 'Clear Filters' text button that appears when any filter is active. (4) When filters result in zero drawings, show: 'No drawings match your filters' with a 'Clear filters' action — distinct from the true-empty state. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's drawing module supports full-screen pan/zoom viewer, side-by-side revision comparison, inline markup tools with cloud/dimension/text, and pin-linked RFIs visible directly on sheets. Fieldwire excels at task-to-plan linking with location pins and Apple Pencil markup. Autodesk ACC has best-in-class BIM coordination with automatic clash detection. SiteSync's current drawings page is essentially a read-only list with no viewer, no markup, no revision comparison, and no upload — it falls dramatically short of all three competitors.",
  "summary": "The document management module scores 23/100, making it the lowest-scoring module in the platform. The API layer (getDrawings) is well-typed and fetches drawings with revisions, but the consuming page lacks fundamental UX: no loading skeletons, no error states, no empty states, no file upload, no discipline filtering, no revision browsing, no drawing viewer, no markup tools, no mobile responsiveness, and no ARIA attributes. The five surgical fixes target the highest-impact gaps: (1) loading/error/empty states to make the page functional, (2) upload UI scaffold so users can add content, (3) ARIA and keyboard accessibility for compliance, (4) responsive layout for field use, and (5) discipline filters and revision display for construction domain depth. These fixes address PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA violations across drawings criteria 1, 6, 7, 8, 10, and global standards for loading, empty, error, responsive, and accessibility."
}
```
